429_pattern.pattern: 6x concentrated in 04:14-04:19 (5min burst), 1x isolated at 03:08, 1x isolated at 04:45

primary_routing_switch.why: 41003 has 7000 deployments (1000 variants × 7 keys) vs 41001's 1792. More deployments = more RPM capacity = less likely all exhaust simultaneously. Data shows 429 insufficient_quota at 04:14-04:19 where all 7 keys quota temporarily exhausted — 7000 dep pool reduces this risk.

config_expansion.why: Single key (KEY1) means no fallback when KEY1 quota exhausted. 7 keys provide 7× fallback depth. Each key has independent quota — KEY1 exhausted → LiteLLM routes to KEY2-7 variants.

41003_resource_increase.why: 7000 deployments require more memory for router state tracking. 2048M provides 33% more headroom. start_period=180s allows slower startup for 7× larger config.

router_settings_unchanged.strategy: simple-shuffle (kept — 7000 dep pool, latency-based-routing overhead not justified)
router_settings_unchanged.why: Data evidence: 429 pattern shows insufficient_quota (not RPM). RateLimitErrorAllowedFails=5 tolerates RPM 429 without cascading cooldown. cooldown_time=10 proportional to RPM window. simple-shuffle distributes uniformly across 7000 dep, maximizing quota utilization.

latency_based_routing_vs_simple_shuffle.observation: latency-based-routing on 1792 dep avg overhead 1.5s (proxy overhead avg 1474ms, median 2ms)
latency_based_routing_vs_simple_shuffle.conclusion: latency tracking overhead not justified for 7000 dep pool. simple-shuffle gives uniform distribution → better quota utilization across all 7 keys.

429_insufficient_quota_handling.conclusion: All 7 keys quota temporarily exhausted. With 7000 dep, exhaustion takes longer (7× more quota per variant). RateLimitErrorAllowedFails=5 prevents premature cooldown on RPM 429.
